name: Mobile phones and market efficiency in developing economies
description: Studies examining whether and how mobile phone access reshapes prices, market coordination, and economic outcomes for producers and traders (e.g. fishing markets, agriculture). Includes both the original impact-evaluation claims and the sociotechnical critiques questioning attribution and context, spanning problem discovery, impact evaluation, and debate over causal interpretation.

name: Community and small-scale cellular/network infrastructure
description: Research on locally owned, sustainable, small-scale connectivity infrastructure such as community cellular networks, low-resource measurement scheduling, and community-currency-funded networks. These span technical design (cs_fundamentals), operations/sustainability, and ethnographic questions of local ownership and governance.

name: Rural electrification via microgrids and minigrids
description: Interventions and engineering studies designing, costing, and piloting standalone microgrids, minigrids, and DC systems for rural and off-grid communities, often in Sub-Saharan Africa, India, and Nepal. Combines technical modeling, multi-criteria decision analysis, operations/sustainability, and field piloting with social impact evaluation.

name: Electricity provision and the political economy of the grid
description: Empirical studies linking electricity supply, blackouts, and distribution losses to political incentives such as election cycles and quasi-experimental policy variation. Spans problem discovery, sensor-based measurement (e.g. crowdsourced blackout mapping), and impact evaluation of governance effects on infrastructure reliability.

name: Satellite/remote-sensing mapping of infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions
description: Machine-learning approaches that infer infrastructure networks, roads, energy assets, poverty, nighttime lights, and urban settlements from satellite, aerial, or drone imagery. These recurringly connect CS methods to development questions about where infrastructure and disadvantaged populations are located.

name: AI for power systems modeling and grid resilience
description: A cluster of ML/optimization work on power flow, cascading-failure mitigation, microgrid simulation, EV penetration, smart-meter analytics, and grid resilience platforms. Predominantly cs_fundamentals with a climate axis, focused on modeling and operating electricity systems under variability and stress.

name: Solar energy forecasting and rooftop/asset assessment
description: Studies using imagery and time-series ML to forecast photovoltaic output (including cloud-cover modeling) and to map solar panels and estimate rooftop solar potential. Recurring across near-term forecasting, oriented object detection, and structured-data-informed potential assessment.

name: Sensing and data-driven optimization of local energy demand and services
description: Work applying sensors and data-driven methods to characterize and optimize household-level energy demand, lighting load profiles, sanitation (latrine sensors), and induced residential electricity demand. Bridges technical modeling with ethnographic and impact-focused framing of last-mile service delivery.

name: Disaster and climate risk detection for infrastructure resilience
description: ML approaches to assess building/infrastructure damage, flood and heat risk, landslide occurrence, waste mapping, and transportation resilience to climate hazards. Recurring pattern of using imagery and simulation to anticipate and respond to climate-exacerbated threats to infrastructure.

name: Infrastructure and design in informal settlements
description: Studies of lighting, construction processes, and built-environment interventions within informal settlements and underserved urban areas, combining engineering assessment with ethnographic and sociotechnical attention to space, culture, and marginalization.

name: Rural telecenters and shared-access digital service delivery
description: Research on shared or assisted digital access points—telecenters and assisted distribution platforms—for underserved populations, focusing on sustainability failures, operations at scale, and the socioeconomic dynamics of intermediated access.

name: Telecom infrastructure and macroeconomic development
description: Macro-level studies estimating the impact of telecommunications penetration and infrastructure investment on economic growth in developing nations. A problem-discovery and impact-evaluation lens on connectivity as a driver of development.
